title: fix: make Websim BYOP compliant
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
- Replaces the inline Websim form with a package-backed React app using `@pollinations/sdk`, `@pollinations/ui`, and `AppUserMenu`.
- Updates the Worker to serve static assets, expose `/api/generate` and `/api/health`, use `openai-fast`, and surface upstream 401/402 errors directly.
- Configures the new Myceli Worker route at `websim.myceli.ai` and deploys version `da179b54-07a9-4e55-ae3e-9ea927042ae7`.
- Makes generation fully BYOP: legacy `GET /{prompt}/` and `/api/generate` now require an incoming bearer token and no longer fall back to app-key or secret Worker credentials.
- Shares one model list between Worker and UI, removes the extra `openai` worker-only model, and keeps only the frontend publishable app key in `config.ts`.
- Refines the responsive layout, centers the empty preview state, removes footer text below panels, matches both panel surfaces, and keeps the primary action labeled `Generate` with auth copy in a shared UI tooltip.
- Moves `Open` into the preview header next to `Save`; both actions appear only once generated HTML is displayed.
- Replaces regex HTML filtering in Websim error text handling with inert DOM parsing to satisfy CodeQL.
- Adds generated app icons/manifest for Websim.

Verification:
- `npx biome check --write apps/websim/worker.js apps/websim/models.js apps/websim/src/config.ts apps/websim/src/App.tsx apps/websim/wrangler.toml apps/websim/README.md`
- `npm run build --prefix apps/websim`
- `npm run typecheck --prefix apps/websim`
- `npx wrangler deploy worker.js --dry-run` from `apps/websim`
- Local Worker: `/api/health` reports `auth: "bearer"` and matching UI model ids.
- Local Worker: unauthenticated `GET /A%20tiny%20calculator/` returns 401.
- Local Worker: unauthenticated `POST /api/generate` returns 401.
- Live `curl https://websim.myceli.ai/api/health` reports `auth: "bearer"` and matching UI model ids.
- Live `curl https://websim.myceli.ai/A%20tiny%20calculator/` returns 401 instead of spending app pollen or returning app-

gist.headline: Websim gets a glow-up
gist.blurb: Websim just got a complete React rewrite. It's now fully integrated with our UI components and runs on your own Pollen balance, making it a proper citizen of the hive.
gist.summary: Websim has been rebuilt as a React app using @pollinations/sdk and @pollinations/ui, introducing a refined responsive layout and standardizing on Bring-Your-Own-Pollen (BYOP) authentication for all generation requests.
gist.impact: Users get a cleaner, centered interface with dedicated 'Save' and 'Open' actions, and can now seamlessly use their own Pollen balance to generate web apps.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee and the Monitor Robot working together to build a website. Polly is inserting a glowing token (representing BYOP/Pollen) into the robot's retro computer setup.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny tool belt, inserting a glowing golden coin into a chunky retro computer terminal. The Monitor Robot companion stands nearby, its screen displaying a tiny colorful website layout. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and peach gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
